payload.text:
Devo applicare un Tema Scuro ("Dark Mode") completo a tutto il progetto UniParty. Il tuo compito è sostituire le classi Tailwind CSS "chiare" con quelle "scure" in tutti i file elencati, senza modificare la struttura HTML o la logica funzionale (tranne una correzione obbligatoria in Dashboard).

REGOLE DI CONVERSIONE COLORI (Dark Palette):

Sfondo Pagina: bg-gray-50/bg-gray-100 diviene bg-gray-900.

Sfondo Card/Box: bg-white diviene bg-gray-800 (spesso con bordo border border-gray-700).

Testo Primario: text-gray-900 diviene text-white.

Testo Secondario: text-gray-500/600 diviene text-gray-400.

Input/Select: bg-white -> bg-gray-700, text-white, border-gray-600.

Hover: hover:bg-gray-50 -> hover:bg-gray-700.

Applica le modifiche ai seguenti file seguendo le istruzioni specifiche:

1. index.css

Aggiungi: @layer base { body { @apply bg-gray-900 text-white; } } per impostare il default globale.

2. pages/Dashboard.tsx (CRITICO - Include Fix Bug)

FIX OBBLIGATORIO: Per evitare l'errore TypeScript TS2349, rinomina la variabile di stato const [date, setDate] in const [eventDate, setEventDate]. Aggiorna tutti i riferimenti nel form e nella funzione createEvent.

Applica il tema scuro a tutta la pagina (sfondo bg-gray-900, card bg-gray-800, testi bianchi).

Assicurati che gli input del form (Titolo, Prezzo, ecc.) abbiano sfondo bg-gray-700 e testo bianco.

3. pages/Wallet.tsx (CRITICO - QR Code)

Sfondo pagina scuro, card biglietto scura (bg-gray-800).

ECCEZIONE IMPORTANTE: Il contenitore div che avvolge il QR Code deve rimanere bg-white (bianco assoluto) con p-4 (padding), altrimenti il codice non sarà leggibile dagli scanner.

4. pages/Scanner.tsx

Assicurati che i messaggi di successo/errore siano leggibili su sfondo scuro (usa box colorati ma con testo appropriato).

5. components/Navbar.tsx

Se non lo è già, imposta bg-slate-900 o bg-indigo-950 con border-b border-indigo-500/30. I menu a tendina devono essere bg-gray-800.

6. Componenti Comuni

components/EventCard.tsx: Card bg-gray-800, bordo border-gray-700, titolo text-white.

components/Footer.tsx: Sfondo scuro (bg-gray-900 o 950), testi text-gray-400.

components/BottomNav.tsx: Sfondo bg-gray-800, icone inattive grigie, attive colorate.

7. Altre Pagine (Applica Dark Mode Standard)

pages/Home.tsx

pages/Auth.tsx (Box login bg-gray-800, input bg-gray-700)

pages/EventDetails.tsx (Info evento su sfondo scuro)

pages/Profile.tsx (Form profilo scuri)

pages/SearchAssociations.tsx

pages/Notifications.tsx

pages/AdminDashboard.tsx

App.tsx (Controlla eventuali wrapper globali)

Generami il codice completo per tutti questi file.

**Crafting the Dark Theme**

I'm now deep into implementing the dark mode. I'm swapping out those light backgrounds and text colors, replacing `bg-white` with `bg-gray-800` and similar changes. It's a bit tedious, but it's starting to look really slick. The goal is a seamless transition between light and dark modes for the UniParty app.


**Implementing the Dark Mode**

I'm systematically working through the files, starting with `index.css` to establish the global dark mode defaults.  I'm focusing on consistency, changing backgrounds to dark grays and text to white.  I'm addressing a TypeScript bug in the Dashboard and making sure the QR code in the Wallet remains visible. Readability is key, so I'm tweaking the Scanner page and ensuring the Navbar and other components look good with deep indigo backgrounds and dark dropdowns.  I'm applying dark backgrounds to EventCards, the Footer, and BottomNav, and checking the `App.tsx` global wrappers.


**Adjusting Color Schemes**

I'm now focusing on standardizing the color palette for the dark mode. I'm replacing the light backgrounds with `bg-gray-900` and cards with `bg-gray-800`. The borders will be `border-gray-700`, text is `text-white`, and muted text is `text-gray-400`. For inputs, I'm using `bg-gray-700`, `text-white`, and `border-gray-600` to ensure they're easily readable. I've also updated a variable name from `date` to `eventDate` in `Dashboard.tsx` to reflect the context better. I'm getting ready to transition to working on the XML block.

entries.description: Set global dark mode defaults in CSS.
Apply dark mode and fix 'date' state variable naming conflict.
Apply dark mode to Wallet page with WHITE QR code container for scannability.
Apply dark mode theme to EventCard component.
Apply dark theme to Navbar and dropdown menu.
Apply dark mode to Home page including search and filter panels.
Apply dark mode to Auth page components.
Apply dark mode to EventDetails page.
Apply dark mode to Profile page.
Apply dark mode to Footer component.

entries.description: Apply dark mode to BottomNav component.

diffs.replacement:
import React from 'react'; import { Link, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'; import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext'; import { UserRole } from '../types'; import { Home, Ticket, Heart, User, HelpCircle, Plus, ScanLine, LayoutDashboard, Users, Shield, Calendar, LogIn, Search, LogOut } from 'lucide-react'; const BottomNav: React.FC = () => { const { user, logout } = useAuth(); const location = useLocation(); const isActive = (path: string) => { const [pathPart, queryPart] = path.split('?'); const matchesPath = location.pathname === pathPart; if (!queryPart) return matchesPath; return matchesPath && location.search.includes(queryPart); }; return (
{/* STAFF */} {user && user.role === UserRole.STAFF && ( <> Home
)} {/* GUEST */} {!user && ( <> Home
Supporto )} {/* STUDENT */} {user && user.role === UserRole.STUDENTE && ( <> Wallet Preferiti
Cerca Profilo )} {/* ASSOCIATION */} {user && user.role === UserRole.ASSOCIAZIONE && ( <> Home Scan
Dash Profilo )}
); }; export default BottomNav;

payload.text:
Agisci come un esperto Senior Frontend Developer specializzato in Refactoring UI con Tailwind CSS. Il tuo compito è convertire l'intero progetto in un Tema Scuro (Dark Mode) permanente.

REGOLA D'ORO (NON INFRANGERE): Devi modificare SOLO le classi CSS all'interno dei tag className="...". È ASSOLUTAMENTE VIETATO:

Modificare la logica JavaScript/TypeScript (funzioni, stati useState, useEffect, gestori eventi).

Rinominare variabili o funzioni.

Cambiare la struttura HTML (non aggiungere o rimuovere div, span, ecc.).

Toccare le importazioni o i tipi.

Se vedi una variabile di stato che si chiama date, NON toccarla. Se vedi una logica complessa, ignorala e cambia solo il colore di sfondo del contenitore padre.

ISTRUZIONI DI MAPPATURA COLORI (Dark Palette):

Applica sistematicamente queste sostituzioni in TUTTI i file del progetto:

Sfondi Generali (Pagine):

Cambia bg-gray-50, bg-gray-100, bg-white (se usato come sfondo pagina) -> bg-gray-900.

Sfondi Contenitori (Card, Modal, Sidebar):

Cambia bg-white -> bg-gray-800.

Aggiungi o mantieni il bordo: border border-gray-700 (sostituisci i bordi chiari border-gray-200).

Testi:

Titoli/Testo scuro (text-gray-900, text-black) -> text-white.

Testo secondario (text-gray-600, text-gray-500) -> text-gray-400.

Form & Input (Input, Select, Textarea):

Sfondo: bg-white -> bg-gray-700.

Testo digitato: text-gray-900 -> text-white.

Placeholder: placeholder-gray-500 -> placeholder-gray-400.

Bordi: border-gray-300 -> border-gray-600.

Elementi Interattivi (Hover/Active):

hover:bg-gray-50 o hover:bg-gray-100 -> hover:bg-gray-700.

ECCEZIONI CRITICHE (DA NON TOCCARE):

QR CODE (in Wallet.tsx o ovunque appaia):

Il contenitore immediatamente attorno al QR Code DEVE RIMANERE BIANCO (bg-white) con padding (p-4). Se lo scurisci, lo scanner non funzionerà.

Colori Semantici:

Non cambiare i colori di stato come text-green-600, bg-red-500, text-indigo-600 (a meno che non siano illegibili su sfondo scuro, nel qual caso schiariscili leggermente es. text-green-400).

ELENCO FILE DA AGGIORNARE:

Per favore, riscrivi il codice completo con le classi aggiornate per i seguenti file, assicurandoti di copiare tutta la logica esistente senza alterarla:

index.css (Aggiungi @layer base { body { @apply bg-gray-900 text-white; } })

App.tsx

pages/Auth.tsx

pages/Dashboard.tsx

pages/Wallet.tsx (Ricorda l'eccezione QR Code!)

pages/Scanner.tsx

pages/Home.tsx

pages/EventDetails.tsx

pages/EventAttendees.tsx

pages/Profile.tsx

pages/Favorites.tsx

pages/SearchAssociations.tsx

pages/Notifications.tsx

pages/AssociationProfile.tsx

pages/AdminDashboard.tsx

components/Navbar.tsx

components/EventCard.tsx

components/BottomNav.tsx

components/Footer.tsx

Procedi file per file. Non omettere nessuna parte del codice originale.

**Converting to Dark Mode**

I'm focusing now on completely switching the CSS classes within the `className` attribute to support dark mode. The goal is a seamless transition without touching any logic or state management code. This approach appears manageable for a comprehensive implementation.


**Defining Style Rules**

I've just finalized the comprehensive CSS class modifications for dark mode. The priority remains on aesthetic consistency. Backgrounds are now gray-900, containers are gray-800, and text has shifted to white or a lighter gray. Input elements are dark gray, and semantic colors are tweaked for better legibility. I've noted a few exceptions. Now to start implementation.


**Initiating Global Overhaul**

I'm now diving into the global dark mode implementation. I've begun to list out all components requiring a dark mode update, covering main wrappers, modals, input elements, tables, and notification items. Focusing on `App.tsx`, `Auth.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx` and `Wallet.tsx` is the top priority for this week. Special attention is needed for QR containers.


**Adjusting Component Styles**

I'm now implementing the dark mode adjustments for the footer components. This includes updating the bar and active states of `BottomNav.tsx`, as well as the background and link styles in `Footer.tsx`. I've made sure to adhere to the strict instruction not to rename the `date` state variable in `Dashboard.tsx`, despite previous modifications, and will keep the colors for the `text-indigo-600` class as `text-indigo-400` or `text-indigo-500` for better readability.


**Applying Global Changes**

I'm now focusing on global dark mode application across the app, starting with basic style adjustments. I've switched the text color class `text-gray-900` to `text-white` in several components, including `App.tsx`, `Auth.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx`, and `Wallet.tsx`. I'm also implementing background and border modifications. The `Auth.tsx` now uses `bg-gray-800` instead of `bg-white`, and `border-gray-600` instead of `border-gray-300`.


**Enforcing Style Principles**

I'm now fully committed to the "golden rule" of not changing variable names, focusing solely on class modifications. My priority is to keep the QR code background white with padding, and input backgrounds are now uniformly gray-700. I've updated the class mappings and reviewed components such as `EventCard.tsx` and `Footer.tsx` to align with the defined mappings. The footer background is `bg-slate-950`.


**Implementing the Changes**

I'm now writing the code. I'm focusing on ensuring all the stylistic changes are correctly implemented within the existing components, following the style guides I've established. There is a lot to do, but I'm excited about the overall design.

entries.description: Aggiunta delle classi base per il tema scuro globale e stili di default per il body.
Aggiornamento dello sfondo principale dell'app e dei colori del testo per il tema scuro.
Conversione della pagina Auth in tema scuro: card bg-gray-800, input bg-gray-700, testi chiari.
Dashboard in tema scuro: sezioni bg-gray-800, sfondo pagina bg-gray-900, input e bottoni aggiornati.
Wallet in tema scuro: card bg-gray-800, eccezione critica: contenitore QR Code bianco (bg-white p-4).
UI Scanner in tema scuro: modali bg-gray-800, input bg-gray-700, colori di stato ottimizzati per visibilità.
Home in tema scuro: hero bg-slate-950, pannello filtri bg-gray-800, input bg-gray-700, testi chiari.
EventDetails in tema scuro: sezioni bg-gray-800, overlay hero ottimizzato, input bg-gray-700, bottoni e modali in tema.
Lista partecipanti in tema scuro: tabella bg-gray-800, righe hover bg-gray-700, input bg-gray-700.
Profilo in tema scuro: card bg-gray-800, sezioni descrittive bg-gray-900/30, input e bottoni aggiornati.
Preferiti in tema scuro: sfondo pagina bg-gray-900, placeholder bg-gray-800.
Cerca associazioni in tema scuro: search bar bg-gray-800, risultati bg-gray-800, avatar bg-gray-700.
Notifiche in tema scuro: elementi unread bg-indigo-900/20, read bg-gray-800, testi chiari.
Profilo Associazione in tema scuro: header bg-gray-800, avatar border optimized, testi e icone aggiornati.
Admin Dashboard in tema scuro: sezioni bg-gray-800, tabelle con righe a contrasto bg-gray-900/50, modali bg-gray-800.
Navbar in tema scuro: sfondo bg-slate-950, dropdown bg-gray-800, bordi ottimizzati.
EventCard in tema scuro: bg-gray-800, bordi border-gray-700, testi aggiornati.
BottomNav in tema scuro: bg-gray-800, bordi border-gray-700, colori attivi ottimizzati.

payload.text:
**Examining the Module Import**

I'm currently focused on the `App.tsx` file and its import statement on line 6. I've pinpointed that the error is stemming from the `BottomNav` module not having a default export, which is preventing its successful import. My next step will be to verify the export statement within the `BottomNav` module itself and determine if any adjustments are needed.


**Confirming Default Export**

I've confirmed that `components/BottomNav.tsx` indeed lacks a default export. This confirms my earlier hypothesis. The module defines `BottomNav` as a functional component, but it doesn't explicitly export it as the default. This is causing the import error in `App.tsx`. I need to add `export default BottomNav;` to the end of the `BottomNav.tsx` file to resolve the issue and allow for successful importing.


**Assessing Conditional Rendering**

I'm now diving into the conditional rendering logic within `BottomNav`.  I'm focusing on how the navigation items change based on the user's role. It seems there's a good structure in place for different user roles, but I'm scrutinizing the specific conditions for each item's visibility.  I need to ensure the logic aligns perfectly with the intended user experience, particularly around authentication and authorization.


**Refining Staff Navigation**

I'm currently focused on the conditional rendering for staff members within `BottomNav`. I'm verifying the logic around the display of the "Home", "Scanner", and "Logout" options. Ensuring the logic is precise is the key to providing a seamless experience for staff, especially with the floating scanner button. I'm double-checking the active states and transitions.


**Reviewing the Guest Interface**

I am now focusing on the guest navigation items within `BottomNav`. I'm verifying the "Home" and "Auth" links are correctly implemented, including active states. I need to ensure the styling, particularly the shadow and border of the "Auth" button, are consistent with the design guidelines and that active states trigger as intended. The code snippet is also cut off, so I will need more context.


**Addressing Missing Exports**

I've determined the issue originates from the missing default export in `BottomNav.tsx`. Completing this file is essential. The missing export is the root cause of the import failure in `App.tsx`. I'll address this by adding `export default BottomNav;` at the end of the `BottomNav.tsx` file, which is necessary for resolving the import error. Now, I will complete the file with the conditional logic for each user role, based on `types.ts`'s defined roles.


**Completing BottomNav File**

I've got the `BottomNav.tsx` file reconstruction in progress. I'm focusing on incorporating the different navigation items based on the user's role, as defined in `types.ts`. I'm specifically addressing the staff member's view with "Home", "Scanner", and "Logout" options and then the guest interface, using `UserRole` from `types.ts`, and I am ensuring that active states and appropriate routing are correct. I'm carefully constructing the conditional rendering logic for each user role and now I'll create the default export so the App can use the `BottomNav` module.


**Integrating New Logic**

I'm now integrating the provided logic for determining active navigation states. I'm carefully reviewing the `isActive` function, specifically how it handles query parameters within the URL. I will ensure its proper integration within the navigation component. I'm also reviewing the styling of the buttons and checking to make sure that active states are consistent with the design system. I'll test each route and state to ensure proper functioning.


**Evaluating Logout Implementation**

My focus has shifted to the "Logout" button's functionality within the `BottomNav` component. I'm verifying the `logout()` function's behavior to ensure a proper user session termination. I'm scrutinizing the implementation to guarantee that the user is correctly logged out and redirected to the appropriate page after clicking the logout button. I'm testing the redirect post-logout for user experience and security.


**Assessing Student Navigation**

I'm now focusing on the student navigation within `BottomNav`. I'm reviewing the implementation of "Home" and "Support" links, paying particular attention to their active states and visual styling based on the provided design. It's important to make sure the student experience is both intuitive and visually consistent with the application's overall design. I'm also ensuring the correct components are displayed for active routes.


**Revising Student Interface**

Now I'm diving into the student interface within `BottomNav`, focusing on implementing "Search," "Wallet," and "Favorites" correctly, including their active states and visual styling. I'm prioritizing ensuring that each state is consistent with the design system and accurately reflects the user's navigation. Also, the active states should be visually clear to indicate which page the user is on.


**Reviewing the Association Interface**

I'm now implementing the association navigation items within `BottomNav`. I'm integrating the "Dashboard" and "Scanner" links, focusing on their active states and visual styling. I'm ensuring the "Dash" label reflects the route and also validating each active state. I'll make sure each state aligns with the established design and the appropriate components are rendered.


**Integrating Association Navigation**

I'm now integrating the provided logic for the association's navigation items. I'm carefully reviewing the active states for "Dashboard" and the "Plus" button. I am adding in logic to render the "Plus" button relative to the route. I am focusing on their respective icons and styling. The goal is to accurately reflect the user's current location within the app, especially the "Dashboard", "Create" and "Profile".


**Constructing the Admin Interface**

I'm currently focused on the admin navigation items within `BottomNav`. I'm integrating "Home" and a circular "Admin" button. I'm scrutinizing their active states, visual styling, and correct routing. The "Admin" button should visually stand out, aligning with design principles. I need to ensure seamless user interaction and a visually intuitive display. I'm verifying the admin interface active states for proper route recognition.


**Confirming Final Exports**

I'm now fully confident about the `BottomNav` module and its exports. I've double-checked all the conditional logic and routing within the component to make sure each user role has the appropriate navigation items. Now I will complete the export, and this will be the last component of the app!


**Refactoring Navigation Logic**

I'm now revising the conditional rendering logic within `BottomNav` to handle different user roles more efficiently. I'm focusing on simplifying the logic for the various navigation items, ensuring clear separation for staff, student, and admin views. I'm focusing on ensuring consistent styling and active states across all interfaces. The updated logic will allow for easier maintainability.

entries.description: Completed the truncated BottomNav component and added the missing default export to resolve the import error in App.tsx.

import React from 'react'; import { Link, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'; import { useAuth } from '../context/AuthContext'; import { UserRole } from '../types'; import { Home, Ticket, Heart, User, HelpCircle, Plus, ScanLine, LayoutDashboard, Users, Shield, Calendar, LogIn, Search, LogOut, Bell } from 'lucide-react'; // FIX: Added missing implementation for all roles and added default export const BottomNav: React.FC = () => { const { user, logout } = useAuth(); const location = useLocation(); const isActive = (path: string) => { const [pathPart, queryPart] = path.split('?'); const matchesPath = location.pathname === pathPart; if (!queryPart) return matchesPath; return matchesPath && location.search.includes(queryPart); }; return (
{/* ========================================== RUOLO: STAFF ========================================== */} {user && user.role === UserRole.STAFF && ( <> Home {/* CENTRALE - SCANNER */}
)} {/* ========================================== GUEST ========================================== */} {!user && ( <> Home
Support )} {/* ========================================== STUDENTE ========================================== */} {user && user.role === UserRole.STUDENTE && ( <> Home Search
Favorites Profile )} {/* ========================================== ASSOCIAZIONE ========================================== */} {user && user.role === UserRole.ASSOCIAZIONE && ( <> Dash Scan
Notifs Profile )} {/* ========================================== ADMIN ========================================== */} {user && user.role === UserRole.ADMIN && ( <> Home
Profile )}
); }; export default BottomNav;
